pattern: Counting how many times each value appears lets you split each count into its pairs and a possible single leftover, which sum to the two requested totals.
hints: Pairs only ever form between equal values, so values of different kinds never interact.
For a value appearing c times you can form c divided by two pairs, and one leftover remains exactly when c is odd.
Tally frequencies, then sum the integer halves for the pair count and sum the parities for the leftover count.

edge_inputs.description: two equal elements form one pair
odd count of one value yields a leftover among full pairs
all distinct so no pairs form

stress.description: random arrays drawn from a small value range to force many collisions
stress.note: values 0..100 over up to 100 elements give frequent duplicates

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def numberOfPairs(self, nums):
        from collections import Counter
        c = Counter(nums)
        pairs = sum(v // 2 for v in c.values())
        leftover = sum(v % 2 for v in c.values())
        return [pairs, leftover]
